mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market triggers recession fears, causing immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in consumer spending and residential construction signals robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above the 2017 baseline indicates capacity utilization is healthy, supporting corporate output and generating positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 100 coincide with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations during mid-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects economic momentum, prompting short-term bullish positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all tested historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above the 310 index threshold forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 310 are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or inflation fails to predict subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth combined with rising retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining INDPRO coincides with positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations during late-cycle expansion periods.
